title: Governorates of Syria

Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Governorates of Syria"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Governorates+of+Syria%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Governorates+of+Syria%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Governorates+of+Syria%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Governorates+of+Syria%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Governorates+of+Syria%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Governorates+of+Syria%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(September 2025)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |  |  |  | | --- | --- | |  | This article needs to be **updated**. Please help update this article to reflect recent events or newly available information. *(September 2025)* |  *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

[Rif Dimashq](/wiki/Rif_Dimashq_Governorate "Rif Dimashq Governorate")  [Quneitra](/wiki/Quneitra_Governorate "Quneitra Governorate")  [Daraa](/wiki/Daraa_Governorate "Daraa Governorate")  [Sweida](/wiki/Sweida_Governorate "Sweida Governorate")  [Homs](/wiki/Homs_Governorate "Homs Governorate")  [Tartus](/wiki/Tartus_Governorate "Tartus Governorate")  [Latakia](/wiki/Latakia_Governorate "Latakia Governorate")  [Hama](/wiki/Hama_Governorate "Hama Governorate")  [Idlib](/wiki/Idlib_Governorate "Idlib Governorate")  [Aleppo](/wiki/Aleppo_Governorate "Aleppo Governorate")  [Raqqa](/wiki/Raqqa_Governorate "Raqqa Governorate")  [Deir ez-Zor](/wiki/Deir_ez-Zor_Governorate "Deir ez-Zor Governorate")  [Al-Hasakah](/wiki/Al-Hasakah_Governorate "Al-Hasakah Governorate") | |

or **counties** in English ([Arabic](/wiki/Arabic_language "Arabic language"): مُحَافَظَة [[muˈħaːfaðˤa]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Arabic "Help:IPA/Arabic"); pl. مُحَافَظَات *muḥāfaẓāt* [[muħafaˈðˤaːt]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Arabic "Help:IPA/Arabic")). The [governorates](/wiki/Governorate "Governorate") are divided into sixty-five [districts](/wiki/Districts_of_Syria "Districts of Syria") ([Arabic](/wiki/Arabic_language "Arabic language"): مناطق, [romanized](/wiki/Romanization_of_Arabic "Romanization of Arabic"): *manāṭiq*, singular *[minṭaqah](/wiki/Mintaqah "Mintaqah")*), which are further divided into subdistricts ([Arabic](/wiki/Arabic_language "Arabic language"): نَاحِيَة [[ˈnaːħijah]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Arabic "Help:IPA/Arabic"), plural نَوَاحِي, *nawāḥī* [[naˈwaːħiː]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Arabic "Help:IPA/Arabic")). The *nawāḥī* contain villages, which are the smallest administrative units.

| **[Damascus](/wiki/Damascus "Damascus")**  [Rif Dimashq](/wiki/Rif_Dimashq_Governorate "Rif Dimashq Governorate")  [Quneitra](/wiki/Quneitra_Governorate "Quneitra Governorate")  [Daraa](/wiki/Daraa_Governorate "Daraa Governorate")  [Sweida](/wiki/Sweida_Governorate "Sweida Governorate")  [Homs](/wiki/Homs_Governorate "Homs Governorate")  [Tartus](/wiki/Tartus_Governorate "Tartus Governorate")  [Latakia](/wiki/Latakia_Governorate "Latakia Governorate")  [Hama](/wiki/Hama_Governorate "Hama Governorate")  [Idlib](/wiki/Idlib_Governorate "Idlib Governorate")  [Aleppo](/wiki/Aleppo_Governorate "Aleppo Governorate")  [Raqqa](/wiki/Raqqa_Governorate "Raqqa Governorate")  [Deir ez-Zor](/wiki/Deir_ez-Zor_Governorate "Deir ez-Zor Governorate")  [Al-Hasakah](/wiki/Al-Hasakah_Governorate "Al-Hasakah Governorate") | |

| Subdivisions | * [District](/wiki/Districts_of_Syria "Districts of Syria") |

[Syria](/wiki/Syria "Syria") is a [unitary state](/wiki/Unitary_state "Unitary state"), but for administrative purposes, it is divided into fourteen **governorates**,[[1]](#cite_note-1) also called **provinces** or **counties** in English ([Arabic](/wiki/Arabic_language "Arabic language"): مُحَافَظَة [[muˈħaːfaðˤa]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Arabic "Help:IPA/Arabic"); pl. مُحَافَظَات *muḥāfaẓāt* [[muħafaˈðˤaːt]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Arabic "Help:IPA/Arabic")). The [governorates](/wiki/Governorate "Governorate") are divided into sixty-five [districts](/wiki/Districts_of_Syria "Districts of Syria") ([Arabic](/wiki/Arabic_language "Arabic language"): مناطق, [romanized](/wiki/Romanization_of_Arabic "Romanization of Arabic"): *manāṭiq*, singular *[minṭaqah](/wiki/Mintaqah "Mintaqah")*), which are further divided into subdistricts ([Arabic](/wiki/Arabic_language "Arabic language"): نَاحِيَة [[ˈnaːħijah]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Arabic "Help:IPA/Arabic"), plural نَوَاحِي, *nawāḥī* [[naˈwaːħiː]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Arabic "Help:IPA/Arabic")). The *nawāḥī* contain villages, which are the smallest administrative units.

Each governorate is headed by a governor, appointed by the [president](/wiki/President_of_Syria "President of Syria"), subject to cabinet approval. The governor is responsible for administration, health, social services, education, tourism, public works, transportation, domestic trade, agriculture, industry, civil defense, and maintenance of law and order in the governorate. The minister of each local administration works closely with each governor to coordinate and supervise local development projects. The governor is assisted by a provincial council, all of whose members are popularly elected for four-year terms. In addition, each council elects from among its members an executive bureau which administers the day to day issues between provincial council sessions. Each executive officer is charged with specific functions.

Districts and subdistricts are administered by officials appointed by the governor. These officials work on local matters with elected district councils and serve as intermediaries between the central government and traditional local leaders, such as village chiefs, clan leaders, and councils of elders.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Governorates_of_Syria&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Upon achieving independence from the French Mandate on April 17, 1946, Syria retained much of the administrative framework inherited from the mandate period and organized its territory into nine top-level districts (later governorates): Aleppo, Damascus, Euphrates (Al-Furat), Hama, Hauran, Homs, Jabal al-Druze, Al Jazira, and Latakia.

During the 1950s, several governorates names were standardized: [Hauran](/wiki/Hauran "Hauran") became [Daraa](/wiki/Daraa_Governorate "Daraa Governorate"), Jabal al-Druze was renamed [Sweida](/wiki/Sweida_Governorate "Sweida Governorate"), and [Al-Jazira](/wiki/Al-Jazira_Province "Al-Jazira Province") became [Al-Hasakah](/wiki/Al-Hasakah_Governorate "Al-Hasakah Governorate").

In the early 1960s, further territorial adjustments were introduced: the Euphrates Governorate was divided into Al-Rashid and [Deir ez-Zor](/wiki/Deir_ez-Zor "Deir ez-Zor"), while [Idlib](/wiki/Idlib_Governorate "Idlib Governorate") Governorate was separated from [Aleppo](/wiki/Aleppo_Governorate "Aleppo Governorate"). In 1962, Damascus was reorganized into two administrative units, Damascus Governorate and the City of Damascus (Madīnat Dimashq).

In 1970, Al-Rashid Governorate was renamed [Raqqa](/wiki/Raqqa_Governorate "Raqqa Governorate").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

On August 27, 1964, [Quneitra Governorate](/wiki/Quneitra_Governorate "Quneitra Governorate") was created from parts of Damascus Governorate. Additional changes occurred in the 1970s, when [Tartus Governorate](/wiki/Tartus_Governorate "Tartus Governorate") was separated from the [Latakia Governorate](/wiki/Latakia_Governorate "Latakia Governorate"). In 1987, the City of Damascus was formally designated as an independent governorate, and Damascus Governorate was reorganized as [Rif Dimashq Governorate](/wiki/Rif_Dimashq_Governorate "Rif Dimashq Governorate"), the rural governorate surrounding the capital, completing the basic structure of Syria's contemporary administrative system.[[3]](#cite_note-3)
